title: Find Score of an Array After Marking All Elements
description:
You are given an array nums consisting of positive integers.

Starting with score = 0, apply the following algorithm:

*   Choose the smallest integer of the array that is not marked. If there is a tie, choose the one with the smallest index.
*   Add the value of the chosen integer to score.
*   Mark the chosen element and its two adjacent elements if they exist.
*   Repeat until all the array elements are marked.

Return _the score you get after applying the above algorithm_.

solutionCode.python:
```python
def calculate_score(nums):
    n = len(nums)
    dp = [0] * (n + 2)

    for i in range(n):
        dp[i + 2] = max(dp[i + 1], dp[i] + nums[i])

    return dp[n + 1]
```
    
    This problem can be solved using dynamic programming. We create an array `dp` with length `n+2` (to handle edge cases) initialized with zeros. We iterate through the given `nums` array and for each element, we store in `dp[i+2]` the maximum value between `dp[i+1]` (if we choose not to pick the current element) and `dp[i] + nums[i]` (if we choose to pick the current element and thus mark the adjacent elements as well).

This way, by the end of the iteration, `dp[n+1]` will store the maximum score possible considering all the elements in the array `nums`.

The time complexity of the solution is O(n), where n is the length of the input array, and the space complexity is also O(n), which is used for the `dp` array.
